- UP: row 36 -> row 33, need (36-33)/3 = 1 UP press

Hmm, the green box ended up at col 9, not col -3. So it seems like the green box hit a boundary (the left edge of the left panel) and stopped.
Actually, looking at the ascii, the left panel starts at col 10 (approximately). The green box is at [36,9]-[38,11], which is at the left edge of the left panel.
So the green box moved LEFT until it hit the left boundary, then moved DOWN. The final position is [36,9]-[38,11].
Now, the left charcoal frame is at [32,11]-[36,15] with interior at [33,12]-[35,14]. The green box is at [36,9]-[38,11], which is below and to the left of the left charcoal frame.
To move the green box to the left charcoal frame interior [33,12]-[35,14]:
- RIGHT: col 9 -> col 12, need (12-9)/3 = 1 RIGHT press
- UP: row 36 -> row 33, need (36-33)/3 = 1 UP press
So I need RIGHT + UP to move the green box to the left charcoal frame.
But wait, the green box is now at [36,9]-[38,11], and the cursor is at [30,30]-[32,32]. The green box is NOT at the cursor position, so the cursor should be selecting the green box NOT at the cursor position, which is... both green boxes are not at the cursor position!
Hmm, let me think about this. There are two green boxes:
1. Green box with c center at [27,45]-[29,47] (in right charcoal frame)
2. Green box with W center at [36,9]-[38,11] (bottom-left)
The cursor is at [30,30]-[32,32], and neither green box is at the cursor position. So which one is selected?
I think the cursor selects the green box that was most recently moved or clicked. Since I just moved the green box with W center, it should still be selected.
Let me try RIGHT + UP to move the green box with W center to the left charcoal frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

# Left charcoal frame interior is at [33,12]-[35,14]
# Need RIGHT (col 9->12) and UP (row 36->33)

action(['RIGHT', 'UP'])
